title: Problem 1 (Bukh)
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $c$ so that for every graph $G$, the expected chromatic number of its random subgraphs satisfies: $$E(\chi(G_{1/2})) > c\,\frac{\chi(G)}{\log \chi(G)}\,?$$
context_text: Boris Bukh asked whether the same $\Theta(n/\log n)$ behaviour known for the chromatic number of a random subgraph of the complete graph $K_n$ occurs for random subgraphs of any $n$-chromatic graph, at least in expectation when $p$ is constant. The problem remains open for the ordinary chromatic number.
paper_contribution: Proves an affirmative answer to a strengthening of Bukh's question for the fractional chromatic number: for every graph $G$ with $\chi_f(G) \geq t$, the fractional chromatic number of $G_p$ is at least $t/(8\log_{1/p}(et)+4)$ with probability at least $1-1/(2t)$.
notes: Reference [5] is a webpage with no publication year; attributed_year set to null. Statement extracted from a clearly readable labelled Problem environment despite PDF source.
paper_title: Fractional chromatic number of a random subgraph
